texto: Who's got a mirror?” I ask. Spider shrugs at me. “We're all fellas. Why would any of us have mirrors?”“I've got a mirror!” Brandon declares happily, digging a compact out of his pocket. No-one is surprised.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Melanie Tushmore que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Who's got a mirror?” I ask. Spider shrugs at me. “We're all fellas. frase_original: Who's got a mirror?” I ask. Spider shrugs at me. “We're all fellas. Why would any of us have mirrors?”“I've got a mirror!” Brandon declares happily, digging a compact out of his pocket. No-one is surprised.

texto: No," Nathan grumbled. "Like, not piss on him, just all around him." Stuart raised an eyebrow. "Nath, you need to chill. We're in a bar, a busy bar. We can't stop people talking to each other." "I know but-""Look, don't worry about it," Stuart insisted. "Try not to turn into a bunny boiler just yet.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Melanie Tushmore: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “No," Nathan grumbled. "Like, not piss on him, just all around him." Stuart raised an eyebrow. "Na…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, hilarious, jealousy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de jealousy.
